message:
feat(installer): integrate generated installers for base category (mjt.5.6)

Adds generated installer hook in setup_filesystem() that routes to
manifest-driven installers when ACFS_USE_GENERATED_BASE=1. Falls
back to legacy installer if disabled.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ht","title":"Implement cloud CLI updates (vercel, wrangler, supabase, fly)","description":"## What
Implement the 'cloud' category for acfs-update:
- Vercel CLI: bun update -g vercel
- Wrangler (Cloudflare): bun update -g wrangler
- Supabase CLI: bun update -g supabase
- Fly CLI: Reinstall via official installer (flyctl self-upgrade)
- AWS CLI: Detect install method, update accordingly

## AWS CLI Handling (added)
AWS CLI can be installed multiple ways:
1. apt-installed: Handled by system updates (apt upgrade)
2. pip-installed: pip install --upgrade awscli
3. Official installer: Re-run installer or use aws --update

Detection: Check `which aws`, then `dpkg -s awscli` vs `pip show awscli`

## Technical Details
- Most cloud CLIs are bun global packages - simple updates
- Fly CLI has built-in: flyctl self-upgrade (or reinstall)
- Cloud CLIs may have authentication state - preserve it

## Considerations
- Only update tools that are installed
- Skip tools without prompting if not present
- For bun packages: bun update -g <pkg>
- Log version changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All cloud CLIs update correctly
- [ ] AWS CLI detected and updated properly
- [ ] Skips uninstalled CLIs
- [ ] Authentication preserved
- [ ] Version changes logged","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:43.995628-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:23.18063-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:23.18063-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in scripts/lib/update.sh:update_cloud() - Wrangler, Supabase, Vercel via bun install -g @latest","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ht","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.514415-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3mp","title":"Implement runtime updates (bun, rust, uv, go)","description":"## What
Implement the 'runtimes' category for acfs-update:
- Bun: bun upgrade
- Rust: rustup update (stable, nightly if installed)
- uv: uv self update (or reinstall via official installer)
- Go: apt handles if system-installed, skip otherwise

## Technical Details
- Each runtime has different update mechanisms
- Bun: ~/.bun/bin/bun upgrade
- Rust: ~/.cargo/bin/rustup update
- uv: May need to source the installer again
- Go: Typically managed via apt, but could be manual install

## Considerations
- Must run as target user, not root (except go via apt)
- Capture before/after versions for each
- Skip if tool not installed (detected by missing binary)
- Rust may update multiple toolchains - capture all

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each runtime updates correctly
- [ ] Skips uninstalled runtimes gracefully
- [ ] Version changes logged
- [ ] Works for both root and non-root users","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:07.808375-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:50.956617-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:50.956617-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented version tracking for bun/rust/uv, added update_go() function","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3mp","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:24.85834-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3nk","title":"Silence Next.js turbopack root warning in monorepo","description":"Next.js build warns about inferred Turbopack workspace root because there are multiple bun.lock files (repo root + apps/web). Configure apps/web/next.config.ts turbopack.root explicitly so Next/Turbopack uses the intended root and the warning goes away (no file deletions).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:30:19.912859-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:34:12.38145-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:34:12.38145-05:00","close_reason":"Set apps/web turbopack.root to the workspace root so Next/Turbopack resolves deps correctly and the monorepo root warning is silenced without deleting any lockfiles.","labels":["dx","web"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4ey","title":"Web Wizard Accounts Step (Pre-Install Guidance)","description":"## Purpose

Add a dedicated wizard step that guides users through account creation BEFORE they run the installer.
This ensures users have everything ready and reduces post-install friction.

## Why Before Install?

1. **Parallel work**: User creates accounts while installer runs
2. **No context switching**: Don't interrupt coding setup to create accounts
3. **Informed decisions**: User understands why each service matters
4. **Reduced abandonment**: Clear checklist = higher completion rate

## User Journey

```
[Provider Selection] → [VPS Setup] → [Accounts & Connections] → [Run Installer]
                                            ↑
                                      NEW STEP
```

## Step Design

### Header
"Set Up Your Accounts"
"These services will supercharge your vibe coding experience"

### Structure
1. **Required** (blocking - must have before proceeding)
   - None! All accounts can be created after. But we strongly encourage:
   
2. **Strongly Recommended** (do these first)
   - Google Account (if using SSO strategy)
   - Tailscale (for remote access)
   - Claude/Anthropic (primary coding agent)
   
3. **Recommended** (high value)
   - GitHub (code hosting)
   - Codex/ChatGPT Pro (secondary agent)
   - Vercel (deployment)
   
4. **Optional** (nice to have)
   - Supabase (database)
   - Cloudflare (CDN)
   - Gemini (third agent)

### Per-Service Card

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Logo] Tailscale                           [✓] Done     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Zero-config VPN for secure remote access                │
│                                                         │
│ Why: Access your VPS from anywhere without exposing     │
│      ports. No firewall config needed.                  │
│                                                         │
│ [Sign up with Google →]  [Other options ▾]              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Google SSO Callout

At the top of the step:
```
💡 Pro Tip: Use the same Google account for all services!
   
   One secure account = simpler logins + better security.
   Make sure your Google account has 2FA enabled.
   
   [Learn more about our security recommendations →]
```

### Progress Tracking

- Checkboxes for each service
- State saved to localStorage
- "Skip for now, I'll set these up later" button
- Summary shows: "3 of 8 accounts ready"

## Technical Implementation

### Service Catalog (lib/services.ts)

```typescript
interface Service {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  logo: string;  // SVG or image path
  category: 'access' | 'agent' | 'cloud' | 'devtools';
  priority: 'required' | 'recommended' | 'optional';
  shortDescription: string;
  whyNeeded: string;
  signupUrl: string;
  googleSsoUrl?: string;  // Direct link to Google SSO signup
  alternativeAuth?: string[];  // e.g., ["GitHub", "Email"]
  postInstallCommand?: string;  // e.g., "sudo tailscale up"
}
```

### AccountsStep Component

- Reads service catalog
- Groups by priority
- Renders ServiceCard for each
- Manages localStorage state
- Shows progress summary

### Navigation

- Can proceed without all accounts (not blocking)
- Show warning if skipping recommended services
- "Skip for now" vs "I've set these up" distinction

## Acceptance Criteria

1. New wizard step appears after VPS setup
2. All target services represented with accurate info
3. Google SSO links work correctly
4. Progress persists across page reloads
5. Mobile-responsive design
6. Accessible (keyboard navigation, screen readers)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:59:04.244899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:59:04.244899-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4ey","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4p7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:14.724796-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4jr","title":"Implement handle_checksum_mismatch() with skip/abort/proceed options","description":"# Task: Implement handle_checksum_mismatch() with batched prompts

## Purpose
When an upstream installer checksum does not match, provide user options while maintaining security.

## Critical UX Improvement: Batch All Mismatches

**Problem with per-tool prompts:**
If 5 tools have changed, user gets 5 separate prompts. Annoying and breaks flow.

**Solution:** Collect all mismatches first, then present ONE decision prompt.

```bash
declare -a CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES=()

# During verification phase (collect, dont prompt)
check_installer_checksum() {
    local tool="$1" url="$2" expected="$3"
    local actual=$(fetch_checksum "$url")
    
    if [[ "$actual" \!= "$expected" ]]; then
        CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES+=("$tool|$url|$expected|$actual")
        return 1  # Mark as mismatch but continue
    fi
    return 0
}

# After all tools checked, present batched decision
handle_all_checksum_mismatches() {
    if [[ ${#CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        return 0  # All good
    fi
    
    echo ""
    log_warn "Checksum mismatches detected for ${#CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES[@]} installers:"
    echo ""
    for entry in "${CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES[@]}"; do
        IFS="|" read -r tool url expected actual <<< "$entry"
        echo "  $tool:"
        echo "    Expected: ${expected:0:16}..."
        echo "    Actual:   ${actual:0:16}..."
    done
    echo ""
    echo "This usually means upstream scripts were updated."
    echo ""
    echo "Options:"
    echo "  [P] Proceed with new versions (update checksums.yaml later)"
    echo "  [S] Skip these tools, install everything else"
    echo "  [A] Abort installation"
    echo ""
    
    read -r -p "Choice [P/s/a]: " choice
    case "${choice,,}" in
        s) 
            for entry in "${CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES[@]}"; do
                SKIPPED_TOOLS+=("${entry%%|*}")
            done
            return 0
            ;;
        a) exit 1 ;;
        *) return 0 ;;  # Proceed (default)
    esac
}
```

## Non-Interactive Mode
In non-interactive (agent/CI) mode, behavior depends on tool classification:
- CRITICAL tool mismatch → abort (cant proceed safely)
- RECOMMENDED tool mismatch → auto-skip with warning

## Security Considerations
- Always log the actual vs expected hash
- Encourage users to verify upstream changes before proceeding
- Document how to update checksums.yaml

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tool classification (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-v8a)
- Used by: verify_and_run() flow (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-anq)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All mismatches collected before any prompt
- [ ] Single batched prompt for all mismatches
- [ ] Clear display of which tools affected
- [ ] P/S/A options with sensible defaults
- [ ] Non-interactive mode follows CRITICAL/RECOMMENDED rules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:37.23646-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:46:15.314163-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:46:15.314163-05:00","close_reason":"Added checksum mismatch batching to security.sh: CHECKSUM_MISMATCHES array, record/clear/count/has functions, handle_all_checksum_mismatches() with P/S/A prompt, check_installer_checksum(), and _handle_mismatches_noninteractive() for CI/agent mode. Integrates with tools.sh for CRITICAL vs RECOMMENDED classification.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4jr","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-v8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:33.143559-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4ou","title":"EPIC: Automated Upstream Checksum Monitoring","description":"# Epic: Automated Upstream Checksum Monitoring

## Overview
Create a GitHub Action that daily monitors upstream installer scripts for changes and auto-creates PRs to update checksums.yaml.

## Problem Statement
Checksums get stale when upstream tools release updates. Users hit 'checksum mismatch' errors that are actually normal releases, not security issues. Maintainers don't check daily; users hit issues first.

## Business Impact
- Stale checksums = false security failures
- Automated PRs enable review (maintainer verifies legitimacy)
- Reduces user friction (fewer 'checksum mismatch' errors)
- Keeps project proactively maintained

## Technical Approach
1. Create .github/workflows/checksum-monitor.yml
2. Daily cron at 6am UTC + manual trigger
3. Run security.sh --verify --json to detect changes
4. If mismatches found, update checksums.yaml
5. Create PR with diff summary and review checklist
6. Optional: Slack/Discord notification for urgent updates

## Success Criteria
- PRs created within 24 hours of upstream changes
- PR body includes old/new checksums and verification checklist
- Maintainer can review and merge with confidence
- No user-facing checksum failures from stale data

## Files to Create/Modify
- .github/workflows/checksum-monitor.yml (new)
- scripts/lib/security.sh: Add --json output for --verify

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 6","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:41:27.836408-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:17:32.064598-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:17:32.064598-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete - all children implemented: GitHub Action workflow (coj), --json verify output (ss3), enhanced PR body (xos)"}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4p7","title":"EPIC: Accounts & Connections (Holistic Service Setup Experience)","description":"## Vision

Transform ACFS from "tool installer" to "complete environment setup" by adding comprehensive 
guidance for account creation, service authentication, and security best practices.

## Problem Statement

Users setting up a VPS for agentic coding face fragmented experiences:
1. **Tool installation** is handled by ACFS ✓
2. **Account creation** - No guidance (user figures it out)
3. **Authentication** - Ad-hoc, service by service
4. **Security posture** - Never explicitly addressed
5. **Connectivity** - Requires manual firewall/port setup

This friction causes:
- Abandoned setups ("I'll finish auth later" → never)
- Security gaps (weak passwords, no 2FA)
- Missed value (tools installed but not connected)
- Access headaches (can't reach VPS reliably)

## Solution: Three-Phase Approach

### Phase 1: Before Install (Web Wizard)
New wizard step: "Accounts & Connections"
- Explains which accounts to create and why
- Prioritizes: required → recommended → optional
- Recommends Google SSO for simplicity
- Provides signup links with SSO pre-selected
- Checklist tracks what user has set up

### Phase 2: During Install
- Tailscale installed automatically (apt repo method)
- Services added to manifest as modules
- Doctor checks verify connectivity

### Phase 3: After Install (Onboard TUI)
- Guided auth flow: "Run these commands to log in..."
- Progress tracking for each service
- Security checklist (2FA enabled? etc.)

## Key Services to Support

### Access Layer (P0 - Required)
- **Tailscale**: Zero-config mesh VPN for secure VPS access

### Coding Agents (P0 - Required)
- **Claude Code**: Primary AI coding agent (Google SSO via claude.ai)
- **Codex CLI**: OpenAI's agent (Google SSO via ChatGPT)
- **Gemini CLI**: Google's agent (native Google account)

### Cloud Platforms (P1 - Recommended)
- **Vercel**: Frontend deployment (Google SSO)
- **Supabase**: Database-as-a-service (Google SSO)
- **Cloudflare**: CDN/DNS/Workers (email-based)

### Developer Infrastructure (P1 - Recommended)
- **GitHub**: Code hosting (link Google email)

## Why Google SSO as Default Recommendation?

### Benefits
1. **Single identity**: One secure account protects all services
2. **Fewer passwords**: Less to remember, less to leak
3. **Strong baseline**: Google's security (2FA, suspicious login alerts) 
   automatically protects all linked services
4. **Robust recovery**: Google account recovery is well-established

### Risk Mitigation
- Single point of failure if Google compromised
- Mitigated by: 2FA + Advanced Protection Program recommendation
- Alternative flows documented for privacy-conscious users

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Web wizard has "Accounts & Connections" step
2. Tailscale installable via ACFS
3. Post-install guidance for service authentication
4. Doctor checks for Tailscale connectivity
5. Security recommendations documented

## Non-Goals (for this epic)

- Automatic account creation (requires OAuth flows)
- Credential storage/management (use native tools)
- Enterprise/team account handling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:56:53.889307-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:56:53.889307-05:00"}
@@ -166,7 +166,7 @@
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6","title":"Phase 5: Testing + CI for manifest-driven installs","description":"## Purpose
Make the refactor safe by adding targeted tests that prevent regressions.

## Test requirements
- Offline curl|bash bootstrap simulation test
- Selection safety tests (dependency closure, skip-dep failure)
- Existing Docker integration tests remain passing
- CI drift check for generated scripts

## Acceptance criteria
- CI fails if generated outputs drift from manifest.
- CI exercises the curl|bash bootstrap code path without network.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:47:14.513673-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:47:14.513673-05:00","labels":["ci","installer","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:47:14.514034-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:47:15.419049-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.1","title":"Add offline curl|bash bootstrap simulation test","description":"## Goal
Prevent regressions in the `curl|bash` bootstrap code path (especially after future refactors).

## Requirements
- Test runs with **no network** by simulating `SCRIPT_DIR=""` while using a **local archive copy**.
- Validate the happy path:
  - bootstrap chooses the archive path
  - extraction succeeds
  - `bash -n` validation runs on sourced scripts
  - sourcing libs + generated scripts works
  - coherence check passes (`acfs.manifest.yaml` matches `ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256`)

Nice-to-have negative coverage (if cheap):
- Break coherence (e.g., swap manifest file) and ensure bootstrap aborts with “bootstrap mismatch”.

## Acceptance criteria
- CI runs this test on every push.
- Failures are crisp and point at bootstrap validation (not later in the install).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:26.303655-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:52:35.799182-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:52:35.799182-05:00","close_reason":"Added offline bootstrap simulation test (tests/vm/bootstrap_offline_checks.sh), wired into tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh and installer CI workflow","labels":["installer","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:26.30408-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.2","title":"Add selection safety tests (--only deps closure, --skip dep failure)","description":"## Goal
Ensure selection logic is predictable, safe, and remains stable as modules evolve.

## Required coverage
- `--only <module>` expands dependencies by default (plan includes deps before the target where needed).
- `--no-deps` disables closure and prints a prominent warning.
- `--skip <dep>` that breaks a required dependency fails early with an actionable error.

## Recommended extra coverage (cheap, prevents footguns)
- Unknown module id in `--only/--skip` fails fast.
- Unknown phase in `--only-phase` fails fast.
- `--print-plan` output is deterministic (stable ordering) and does not mutate state.
- Legacy flags (e.g., `--skip-vault`) map into the same selection engine (at least one golden test).

## Acceptance criteria
- Tests encode the “golden path” examples from `mjt.1.2` so docs/spec can’t drift.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:26.466872-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:08:32.800456-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:08:32.800456-05:00","close_reason":"Added 5 missing tests: unknown phase, print-plan determinism, legacy flag mapping (vault, postgres, cloud). All 15 tests pass.","labels":["installer","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:26.467226-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:26.771646-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.3","title":"Update CI workflows for manifest sync + bootstrap simulation","description":"## Goal
Make drift and bootstrap regressions impossible to merge.

## Requirements
- Drift enforcement:
  - CI runs generator and fails if `scripts/generated/**` differs from what the manifest produces.
  - Prefer using generator `--validate/--diff` workflows once available.

- Shell safety checks:
  - `bash -n` on sourced scripts (libs + generated).
  - ShellCheck on installer scripts (where applicable).

- Installer path coverage:
  - Run the offline `curl|bash` bootstrap simulation test (`mjt.6.1`).
  - Run selection safety tests (`mjt.6.2`).

## Acceptance criteria
- CI blocks merges on any drift, syntax error, or bootstrap regression.
- Bootstrap simulation runs without network so it’s reliable in CI.

+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.6.3","title":"Update CI workflows for manifest sync + bootstrap simulation","description":"## Goal
Make drift and bootstrap regressions impossible to merge.

## Requirements
- Drift enforcement:
  - CI runs generator and fails if `scripts/generated/**` differs from what the manifest produces.
  - Prefer using generator `--validate/--diff` workflows once available.

- Shell safety checks:
  - `bash -n` on sourced scripts (libs + generated).
  - ShellCheck on installer scripts (where applicable).

- Installer path coverage:
  - Run the offline `curl|bash` bootstrap simulation test (`mjt.6.1`).
  - Run selection safety tests (`mjt.6.2`).

## Acceptance criteria
- CI blocks merges on any drift, syntax error, or bootstrap regression.
- Bootstrap simulation runs without network so it’s reliable in CI.

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.7","title":"Phase 6: Documentation + maintainer workflow","description":"## Purpose
Document the *new* contract so future changes don’t reintroduce drift.

## Scope
- Update README in existing sections (no new “contributing” content).
- Add an internal maintainer guide for adding/updating modules safely.
- Update wizard/docs to prefer tagged releases for curl|bash installs.

## Acceptance criteria
- Docs match reality (no “two universes”).
- Internal-only posture preserved.

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.7.2","title":"Write internal maintainer guide: adding modules + regenerating safely","description":"## Goal
Future-proof: make it easy to add modules without breaking invariants.

## Must cover
- Adding module fields correctly (phase/run_as/installed_check/verify)
- Adding checksums
- Running generator + drift checks
- How to validate curl|bash bootstrap changes

## Constraints
- Internal-only posture; no “contributing” language.
